mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) relative to baseline during rate decline periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 5.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the index of industrial production during late-cycle expansions signal capacity constraints and impending economic slowdown, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle expansions do not precede negative 5-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 0.5 percent signals restrictive monetary policy that increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in over 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls alongside a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic output and corporate earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns with a win rate above 52% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Improvements in the index of industrial production during post-shock recovery phases signal expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during post-shock recovery periods do not precede positive 20-day returns with a t-statistic greater than 1.96.
